title: Pairwise Xors

content.problemStatement:
JJ gives Chef a number XXX and challenges Chef to come up with three **distinct** numbers A,A,A, B,B,B, and CCC such that:


0≤A,B,C<2300 \le A, B, C \lt 2^{30}0≤A,B,C<230;
(A⊕B)+(B⊕C)+(C⊕A)=X(A \oplus B) + (B \oplus C) + (C \oplus A) = X(A⊕B)+(B⊕C)+(C⊕A)=X.
Help Chef come up with three such numbers or determine that no such tuple exists.

Here, ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the bitwise XOR operation.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integer XXX - the number mentioned in the problem statement.


Output Format
For each test case, output three **distinct** numbers AAA, BBB and CCC (0≤A,B,C<230)(0 \le A, B, C \lt 2^{30})(0≤A,B,C<230) such that (A⊕B)+(B⊕C)+(C⊕A)=X(A \oplus B) + (B \oplus C) + (C \oplus A) = X(A⊕B)+(B⊕C)+(C⊕A)=X.


If multiple such tuples exist, print any. If no such tuple exists, print −1-1−1.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X (0 ≤ X < 2^30).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output three integers A, B and C (0 ≤ A, B, C < 2^30) such that (A⊕B)+(B⊕C)+(C⊕A)=X. If no such triple exists output -1 -1 -1. Separate answers for different test cases by a newline.

sampleTestCases.explanation: X=6 is even, Y=3 → (0,3,3) gives 3+0+3=6. X=3 is odd → impossible. X=20 is even, Y=10 → (0,10,10) gives 10+0+10=20.
X=1 is odd, no triple can satisfy the condition.

solution.approach: For any three numbers the sum (A⊕B)+(B⊕C)+(C⊕A) is always even because each bit contributes either 0 or 2 to the sum. Therefore an odd X can never be represented and the answer is -1 -1 -1. If X is even, let Y = X/2. The triple (0, Y, Y) satisfies the equation because (0⊕Y)=Y, (Y⊕Y)=0, (Y⊕0)=Y and Y+0+Y = 2·Y = X. This construction works for all even X and respects the required bounds. The algorithm is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) overall, O(1) per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) besides input and output storage.
